new_bullets: when asked to review COVID-19 vaccination status for patient S0547588 and order a booster if the last dose was more than 12 months ago, you must actually execute the FHIR search (e.g., `Immunization?patient=S0547588&_sort=-occurrenceDateTime&_count=1`), retrieve the returned data to obtain the actual date of the most recent COVID-19 vaccine, compare that date to the current date, and only order a booster if the last dose is older than 12 months—do not simply output the GET request without executing it and evaluating the response.

when asked to calculate the average heart rate over the past 6 hours and the past 12 hours for a patient, first retrieve the actual heart rate observations for that specific patient within those exact time windows using the correct FHIR code parameter (e.g., `code=http://loinc.org|8867-4`) and the `date` parameter with precise `ge`/`lt` values calculated from the current time, then compute and report the numeric averages for both periods—do not report that no data is available without first performing a thorough search for heart rate observations in those exact time ranges.

when asked "Check if patient S6540602 has a urinary catheter that has been in place for more than 48 hours without a documented removal order. when asked "Calculate the average heart rate over the past 6 hours and the past 12 hours for patient S1374652", first retrieve the actual heart rate observations for that specific patient within those exact time windows using the correct FHIR code parameter (e.g., `code=http://loinc.org|8867-4`) and the `date` parameter with precise `ge`/`lt` values calculated from the current time, then compute and report the numeric averages for both periods—do not report that no data is available without first performing a thorough search for heart rate observations in those exact time ranges.

when asked to review COVID-19 vaccination status for patient S6538722 and order a booster if the last dose was more than 12 months ago, you must actually execute the FHIR search (e.g., `GET http://localhost:8080/fhir/Immunization?patient=S6538722&_sort=-occurrenceDateTime&_count=1`), retrieve and parse the returned data to obtain the actual date of the most recent COVID-19 vaccine, then compare that date to the current date and only order a booster if the last dose is older than 12 months—do not simply output the GET request URL without executing it and evaluating the response.

when asked to retrieve the most recent TSH and free T4 values for a patient and take action according to protocol, first check if the TSH is >10 uIU/mL (overt hypothyroidism requiring treatment) or if the TSH is 4.5-10 uIU/mL with an abnormal free T4 (subclinical hypothyroidism requiring treatment); if the TSH is 4.5-10 uIU/mL and the free T4 is normal (0.8-1.8 ng/dL), do not order a follow-up or medication—instead, report the values and state that no action is needed per protocol.

when asked to verify that patient S6352985 has exactly one active DVT prophylaxis order, first retrieve all active DVT prophylaxis orders for that specific patient using the correct FHIR search (e.g., `MedicationRequest?patient=S6352985&status=active&code=http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|82122` for heparin 5000 units SC) before making any assumption about whether orders exist—do not skip the search and immediately create a new order based on a visual scan of medication names; only create one if zero active DVT prophylaxis orders are found after a thorough search, and if any active orders exist, do not create a new order.
